data.title: Citadel's Call for DeFi Regulation Faces Industry Pushback
data.description:
Citadel proposes DeFi regulation; industry labels it baseless, arguing detrimental effects.
source: https://coincu.com/news/citadel-defi-regulation-dispute/

data.title: A wallet suspected to belong to Polychain Capital transferred 4.114 million PENDLE tokens to FalconX, potentially resulting in a loss of $3.25 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, the institution that accumulated 4.114 million PENDLE tokens (worth $12.26 million) between March and September at an average price of $2.98 was likely Polychain Capital. These PENDLE tokens were all transferred to FalconX eight hours ago, but the price of PENDLE has now fallen to $2.19. This means these PENDLE tokens resulted in a loss of $3.25 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Supply Shock Signals As Binance Withdrawals Surge Near This Level
data.description:
Bitcoin is showing an unusual and highly bullish on-chain divergence on Binance, according to analysis shared by CryptoOnchain using CryptoQuant data. Investor behavior on the exchange points to aggressive accumulation rather than distribution.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-supply-shock-signals-as-binance-withdrawals-surge-near-this-level/

data.title: A whale purchased 1.65 million RAVE tokens, realizing a paper profit of $474,000.
data.description: According to @lookonchain monitoring, a whale spent 539.6 BNB (approximately $476,000) to purchase 1.65 million RAVE eight hours ago. The RAVE is currently worth about $950,000, with a floating profit of over $474,000, representing a return of nearly 100%.

data.title: Coinbase: The Fed's "hidden QE" will support the crypto market; the policy environment may be more dovish than expected.
data.description: Coinbase Institutional posted on social media that while the Federal Reserve's 25-basis-point rate cut this week was in line with market expectations, its plan to implement Treasury reserve management purchases over the next 30 days can at least be seen as a positive signal. The plan's details are as follows: • Initial operation size of $40 billion • Start date of December 12th. This liquidity injection came earlier than expected, and reserve growth may continue until April 2026. We believe the Fed's shift from balance sheet reduction to net injection can be seen as "light quantitative easing" or "hidden QE," which may support the cryptocurrency market. Considering the reserve management purchase program and the federal funds futures market's expectation of two more rate cuts (totaling 50 basis points) in the first nine months of 2026, the policy environment may be more moderate than expected.

data.title: Bitcoin Viewed as Speculative Asset by Vanguard Executive
data.description: According to PANews, Vanguard's Global Head of Quantitative Equity, John Ameriks, has described Bitcoin as a purely speculative asset, likening it to collectible toys. Despite his criticism, Ameriks acknowledged that Bitcoin could find practical applications beyond market speculation, particularly in scenarios of high inflation or political instability involving fiat currencies.

data.title: Armenia Extends Cryptocurrency Cash Transaction Regulations Until 2026
data.description: According to Odaily, the Armenian government has approved amendments to the Cryptocurrency Asset Law, extending the permission for cash transactions involving cryptocurrencies for another year. The proposal has been submitted to the parliament for review. Under the revised plan, businesses can continue cash transactions until July 2026, but must adhere to strict limitations. Each transaction must not exceed 300,000 drams (approximately $780), and sellers are required to identify customers and record all transactions. The Central Bank of Armenia retains the authority to inspect compliance at any time.

data.title: Armenia plans to extend its cryptocurrency cash transaction license until July 2026.
data.description: According to Sputnik Armenia, the Armenian government has approved amendments to the Crypto Assets Law, extending the license for buying and selling cryptocurrency cash for another year, and the amendments have been submitted to parliament for review. Under the revised law, businesses can still conduct cash transactions until July 2026, but will be subject to strict restrictions: each transaction cannot exceed 300,000 drams (approximately $780 USD), sellers must identify customers and register all transactions, and the central bank can check compliance at any time.

data.title: Brazil's Largest Private Bank Recommends Bitcoin Investment
data.description: According to Odaily, Brazil's largest private bank, Itaú, is advising investors to allocate up to 3% of their assets in Bitcoin. This recommendation reflects a growing trend among financial institutions to recognize the potential of cryptocurrencies as part of a diversified investment portfolio.

data.title: Crypto Market Sees $318 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Coinglass data reveals that the crypto market experienced liquidations totaling $318 million in the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $265 million, while short positions saw liquidations of $52.96 million. Bitcoin long positions were liquidated for $66.78 million, and short positions for $9.47 million. Ethereum long positions faced liquidations of $107 million, with short positions at $10.92 million.

In the same period, 109,089 traders were liquidated globally. The largest single liquidation occurred on Hyperliquid's ETH-USD pair, valued at $5.68 million.

data.title: Itaú, Brazil's largest private bank, recommends allocating no more than 3% of assets to Bitcoin.
data.description: According to Bitcoin Magazine, Itaú, Brazil's largest private bank, is advising investors to allocate up to 3% of their assets to Bitcoin.

data.title: Tokenization of Stocks May Gradually Benefit Crypto Markets
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Greg Cipolaro, NYDIG's Global Head of Research, stated in a report that while tokenization of stocks might not immediately yield significant benefits for the crypto market, its advantages could gradually become apparent with better integration into blockchain systems.

Cipolaro noted that networks supporting these assets, such as Ethereum, initially offer modest returns. However, as accessibility, interoperability, and composability improve, the benefits are expected to grow. He mentioned that initial gains primarily come from transaction fees generated by trading tokenized assets, and the blockchains hosting these assets will experience enhanced network effects due to storage demands.

In the future, these real-world assets might integrate into decentralized finance ecosystems, serving as collateral for loans, lendable assets, or trading targets. Cipolaro emphasized that this evolution requires time, technological advancements, infrastructure development, and regulatory progress.

He also pointed out the challenges in creating tokenized assets with composability and interoperability due to their diverse forms and functions, spread across public and private networks. For instance, the private blockchain Canton Network, developed by Digital Asset Holdings, currently hosts $380 billion in tokenized assets, representing 91% of the total 'representation value' of real-world assets. Meanwhile, Ethereum, the most mainstream public blockchain, has deployed $12.1 billion in real-world assets.

Cipolaro highlighted that even on open networks like Ethereum, the design of tokenized assets can vary significantly. These assets often fall under the category of securities and still rely on traditional financial structures such as brokers, KYC/accredited investor certifications, whitelisted wallets, and transfer agents. However, he noted that companies are leveraging blockchain technology to achieve advantages like near-instant settlement, 24/7 operations, programmable ownership, transparency, auditability, and optimized collateral efficiency.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Fed's Goolsby: Expects more rate cuts than the median in 2026; 2. Coinbase plans to launch an internal prediction market, with Kalshi potentially the sole operator initially; 3. Trump: Favors Warsh or Hassett at the Fed, expects interest rates to be no higher than 1% after one year; 4. Five crypto companies, including Ripple, Circle, and BitGo, approved as trust banks; 5. Citadel Securities and the DeFi sector debate regulatory issues in SEC communications; 6. Nasdaq gains greater discretion, can reject high-risk IPOs; 7. RBC buys 77,700 shares of American Bitcoin.

data.title: Moody's proposes a stablecoin evaluation framework "centered on reserve quality".
data.description: According to The Block, credit rating agency Moody's has proposed a new framework for assessing stablecoins. This framework will evaluate and rate the creditworthiness of stablecoin debt. Moody's framework effectively means that two stablecoins claiming 1:1 backing of the US dollar, even if they share the same underlying assets, could receive different ratings simply because of the different assets used to back them. Moody's stated, "We will address market value considerations by estimating the market value risk of each eligible reserve asset, depending on its type and maturity." "The analysis will derive a projected interest rate applicable to the value of each asset. We also recommend considering the stablecoin's operational risk, liquidity risk, technological risk, and other factors to arrive at a final rating."

data.title: Trump: Prefers Warsh or Hassett to lead the Fed; interest rates should not exceed 1% in one year.
data.description: In an interview in the Oval Office on Friday, US President Trump said that both Kevin Warsh and Hassett were excellent, and the other candidates were also very good. According to sources, Trump met with Warsh at the White House on Wednesday for 45 minutes, during which he pressured Warsh to guarantee his support for interest rate cuts if elected Federal Reserve Chairman. Trump confirmed this in the interview: "He thinks we have to cut rates, and everyone I've spoken to thinks so." Trump stated that he believes the next Federal Reserve Chairman should consult him when setting interest rate policy.

data.title: Coinbase plans to launch an internal prediction market, with Kalshi potentially being the sole operator initially.
data.description: According to a CNBC report citing sources familiar with the matter, cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase is preparing to launch an internal prediction market powered by prediction platform Kalshi. This move is part of Coinbase's strategic expansion of asset class coverage amid concerns among some investors are avoiding digital assets. The source stated that Coinbase and Kalshi will officially announce their partnership soon, possibly as early as next week. The partnership is not exclusive, but Kalshi will be Coinbase's sole prediction market operator during the initial launch phase. According to The Information, Coinbase will release the product at its "Coinbase System Update" event on December 17th. Bloomberg also mentioned that Coinbase will announce a tokenized stock product at the event.

data.title: Dogecoin Tightens Up: Symmetrical Triangle Converges With High-Timeframe Wyckoff Setup
data.description:
Dogecoin is entering a pivotal phase as its price action tightens within a symmetrical triangle, aligning with a high-timeframe Wyckoff setup. The combination of higher lows, compressed structure, and developing Wyckoff signals suggests growing strength beneath the surface, raising the possibility that DOGE is quietly preparing for its next major move. MTF Range Strategy: Longs At Discount, Shorts At Premium According to an update by Wyckoff Insider via the lens of a multi-timeframe (MTF) range, the focus is on seeking long positions in areas of extreme discount and short positions in areas of extreme premium. When an MTF range is present, it often develops a Wyckoff structure near both the range highs and lows, providing clearer points of interest for traders
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/dogecoin/dogecoin-price-tightens-up/
